lenses.guidance: 'Kingdom of priests and holy nation' (v 6) — Hebrew mamleket kohanim. Each Israelite is priest by vocation; the nation as a whole mediates God's presence outward. The original audience hears corporate priesthood.

The theophany structure escalates — three days of consecration (vv 10-11), boundary lines (v 12), trumpet blast (v 16), smoke and fire (v 18), shaking mountain (v 18). The narrative pace is liturgical preparation.

Sinai's fire-and-smoke theophany (vv 16-19) is the type Hebrews 12:18-24 names — and Mount Zion (Heb 12:22) is the antitype. The pattern: terrified-distance at Sinai becomes confident-approach at the cross.

God descends to the mountain to meet his people (v 20). The pattern of God-comes-down anchors at the incarnation: 'the Word became flesh and dwelt among us' (Jn 1:14). Sinai's descent becomes Bethlehem's.

v 6's 'kingdom of priests and holy nation' is cited at 1 Peter 2:9 — applied directly to the church. The Sinai vocational identity carries across Scripture: Israel-then becomes the church-now without remainder.
